title: Migration Slice Loop
summary: Executes risky migrations through thin reversible slices with verification at each boundary.
steps: Define the old behavior, new behavior, compatibility window, rollback point, and safety checks.
Choose the thinnest vertical slice that proves the migration pattern end to end.
Ship compatibility scaffolding before moving callers or data.
Move one slice, verify dual-read or equivalent correctness where practical, and keep rollback possible.
Retire old paths only after all slices are verified and observability shows no drift.
stopConditions: migration complete and old path removed
drift or rollback risk exceeds threshold
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Migration Slice Loop for [migration_goal, slice_size, rollback_plan].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Define the old behavior, new behavior, compatibility window, rollback point, and safety checks.
- Choose the thinnest vertical slice that proves the migration pattern end to end.
- Ship compatibility scaffolding before moving callers or data.
- Move one slice, verify dual-read or equivalent correctness where practical, and keep rollback possible.
- Retire old paths only after all slices are verified and observability shows no drift.

Quality gates before completion:
- slice is independently verifiable
- rollback path preserved
- data correctness checked
- old and new paths monitored

Stop when one of these is true:
- migration complete and old path removed
- drift or rollback risk exceeds threshold
- approval-gated step reached

Finish with:
- migration plan
- slice checklist
- verification output
- rollback notes

Escalate for human approval before: data deletion, schema destructive changes, public API removal.
